If there is an ant-hill darkened by the holy grass to the north-
east of a mountain ebony tree, there will be inexhaustible water at a
depth of 22| cubits between the tree and the ant-hill. The indications
mentioned are a snake of the colour of the lotus-calix at a depth of 5
cubits; below that red earth; and lower still a coryndon stone (ruby).
[In the first line is another reading. But in our previ-
ous edition we had adopted the reading given here. This is more
sensible than the one where fef appears instead of iifiHd]-

Should an Echites tree be covered by ant-hills, it should be pre-
dicted that there is water at a depth of 25 cubits at a distance of one
cubit to its north.
